landingPage.heroTitle: Your Journey. Your Rewards. Our Shared Future.
landingPage.heroSubtitle: Your 2026 Total Rewards program is designed to simplify and enhance your experience — built around your feedback and your unique needs.

explainerVideo.title: 2026 Benefits Overview
explainerVideo.description: Watch a quick overview of your RS&H benefits for 2026.

enrollmentChecklist.title: Review your benefit options
enrollmentChecklist.description: Read through the 2026 Benefits Guide and use ALEX at start.myalex.com/rsh to get personalized guidance on which plans fit your needs.

enrollmentChecklist.title: Log in to the enrollment portal
enrollmentChecklist.description: Visit Blaze and select the UKG icon, then navigate to Menu > Myself > Open Enrollment.

enrollmentChecklist.title: Elect your medical plan
enrollmentChecklist.description: Choose from PPO Plan, Prime HDHP, or Alternate HDHP. Consider pairing an HDHP with an HSA for triple tax advantages.

enrollmentChecklist.title: Elect dental and vision coverage
enrollmentChecklist.description: Select Core or Enhanced dental through MetLife, and Core VSP or Enhanced VSP for vision. These can be elected independently of medical.

enrollmentChecklist.title: Set up your HSA or FSA contributions
enrollmentChecklist.description: If enrolling in Prime or Alternate HDHP, establish your HSA contribution via Fidelity. PPO enrollees may elect a Healthcare FSA up to $3,400.

enrollmentChecklist.title: Review voluntary and supplemental benefits
enrollmentChecklist.description: Consider Voluntary Life, Accident, Critical Illness, Short-Term Disability Buy-Up, Pet Insurance, Legal Plan, and Identity Theft Protection.

enrollmentChecklist.title: Designate your beneficiaries
enrollmentChecklist.description: Update your life insurance and AD&D beneficiary designations in UKG.

enrollmentChecklist.title: Confirm your 401(k) contribution
enrollmentChecklist.description: You are auto-enrolled at 6%. Log in to NetBenefits.com to adjust your contribution or investment elections.

retirementPlanning.heroTitle: Build Your Future with the RS&H 401(k)
retirementPlanning.heroDescription: The RS&H 401(k) plan lets you contribute pre-tax or Roth dollars toward retirement, with a 50% company match on up to 6% of your pay.

features.title: Company Match
features.description: RS&H matches 50% of your contributions up to 6% of pay — that's free money toward your retirement.

features.title: Auto-Enrollment at 6%
features.description: New associates are automatically enrolled at 6% of pay into T. Rowe Price Target Date Funds.

features.title: Employee Ownership
features.description: 1/3 of the company match is contributed in RS&H company stock — growing your stake in the firm.

features.title: 5-Year Vesting Schedule
features.description: Become 100% vested in company contributions after 5 years of service.

sections.title: Standard Contribution
sections.content: Up to $24,500 for associates under age 50. Contribute to traditional 401(k), Roth 401(k), or a combination.

sections.title: Catch-Up Contribution (Age 50–59)
sections.content: Up to $32,500 total annual contribution for associates ages 50–59.

sections.title: Super Catch-Up (Age 60–63)
sections.content: Associates ages 60–63 may contribute up to $35,750 under the SECURE 2.0 super catch-up provision.

sections.title: Rollover
sections.content: You may roll over funds from a prior employer's 401(k) into the RS&H plan. Contact Fidelity at 1-800-835-5095.

chapters.title: General Information
chapters.description: Eligibility rules, enrollment deadlines, and how to make benefit changes at RS&H.
contentParagraphs: Benefits are available to all full-time and part-time associates working a minimum of 20 hours per week, and their eligible dependents.
If you enroll during Open Enrollment, your benefits become effective January 1, 2026. New hires are effective the first day of the month following your date of hire.
You may only change elections during Open Enrollment or within 30 days of a qualifying life event (marriage, birth/adoption, loss of other coverage, etc.).

sections.title: Eligible Dependents
paragraphs: Spouse or Domestic Partner
Children from birth to age 26 (natural, legally adopted, stepchildren)
Unmarried dependent children of any age who are mentally or physically disabled and dependent on you

sections.title: How to Enroll
paragraphs: Visit Blaze and select the UKG icon
Navigate to Menu > Myself > Open Enrollment
New hires must enroll within 10 days of hire via Menu > Myself > Life Events > I am Enrolling in Benefits

sections.title: Qualifying Life Events
paragraphs: Birth, legal adoption, or placement for adoption
Change in marital status
Dependent child reaches age 26
Spouse gains or loses employment or eligibility
Death of a covered dependent
Spouse or dependent becomes eligible/ineligible for Medicare/Medicaid
Change in residence that changes eligibility
Court-ordered change

rows.label: New Hire
cells: 1st day of the month following hire date

rows.label: Qualifying Life Event
cells: Within 30 days of the life event

chapters.title: Medical & Prescription Drug
chapters.description: Compare RS&H's three BlueCross BlueShield medical plans — PPO, Prime HDHP, and Alternate HDHP — including deductibles, copays, and prescription drug coverage.
contentParagraphs: Your medical benefits are provided by BlueCross BlueShield and include coverage for both in-network and out-of-network providers. All plans participate in the same network.
Use the My Health Toolkit app or myhealthtoolkitfl.com to estimate costs, manage claims, and find providers.
Not sure which plan is right for you? Use ALEX at start.myalex.com/rsh for personalized, confidential guidance.

chapters.title: Dental
chapters.description: MetLife dental plans with in-network and out-of-network coverage for preventive, basic, and major services.
contentParagraphs: Dental plans are provided through MetLife and cover diagnostic/preventive care, basic, and major services. You can use any dentist, but in-network providers save you more.
To find an in-network dentist, visit www.metlife.com/dental or use MetLife's mobile app.
With a MetLife Plus dentist: no balance billing, no pre-payment required, quality assurance monitoring, and lower negotiated prices.

chapters.title: Vision
chapters.description: VSP vision plans covering eye exams, frames, lenses, and contacts — with discounts on LASIK.
contentParagraphs: Vision benefits are provided through Vision Service Plan (VSP) and cover eye exams, lenses, frames, contact lenses, and laser surgery discounts.
In-network providers offer higher benefits at lower cost. Visit www.vsp.com to find a provider.
Associates can elect dental and/or vision regardless of their medical enrollment status.

chapters.title: Associate Payroll Contributions
chapters.description: Bi-weekly premium amounts for medical, dental, and vision coverage based on coverage tier.
contentParagraphs: The following are bi-weekly (per paycheck) payroll deductions for medical, dental, and vision coverage.

chapters.title: Health Savings Account (HSA)
chapters.description: Triple tax-advantaged savings account available with Prime HDHP and Alternate HDHP enrollment.
contentParagraphs: A Health Savings Account (HSA) is paired with a High Deductible Health Plan (HDHP) and provides a triple tax advantage: contributions are tax-free, interest earned is tax-free, and withdrawals for qualified health expenses are tax-free.
Access and manage your HSA through your Fidelity NetBenefits account at NetBenefits.com.
Unspent HSA dollars roll over each year and are yours to keep — even if you retire or leave RS&H. You can invest your HSA funds for long-term growth.

sections.title: HSA Eligibility Requirements
paragraphs: Enrolled in Prime HDHP or Alternate HDHP
Not covered by a spouse's non-HDHP plan
Not enrolled in Medicare, TRICARE, or TRICARE for Life
No one else can claim you as a dependent
Have not received VA benefits in the past 3 months

chapters.title: Flexible Spending Account (FSA)
chapters.description: Tax-advantaged accounts to pay for eligible healthcare, dependent care, transit, and parking expenses with pre-tax dollars.
contentParagraphs: A Flexible Spending Account (FSA) lets you set aside pre-tax dollars to pay for eligible healthcare or dependent care expenses.
Eligible claims must be incurred between January 1 and December 31. All claims must be submitted by March 15.
Up to $660 of unused Healthcare FSA funds may roll over to the new plan year. Any unused amount above $660 is forfeited.
HDHP enrollees must use a Limited Purpose FSA (dental and vision expenses only).

rows.label: Eligible Expenses
cells: Medical, dental, vision, and other IRS-qualified expenses
Dependent care for children under 13, disabled dependents, elderly parents in-home

chapters.title: Transit and Parking FSA
chapters.description: Pre-tax accounts to pay for eligible commuting and work parking expenses.
contentParagraphs: A Transit FSA lets you use pre-tax dollars to pay for mass transit commuting expenses (train, bus, ferry). Contribute up to $340 per month in 2026.
A Parking FSA lets you pay for eligible work-related parking expenses (ramp or park-and-ride) with pre-tax dollars.
You may enroll in Transit or Parking FSAs at any time during the year — no qualifying event required.
Manage and access reimbursements through your Fidelity NetBenefits account at NetBenefits.com. Note: IRS regulations do not permit reimbursements for expenses older than 180 days.

sections.title: Ineligible Transit Expenses
paragraphs: Tunnel, bridge, or highway tolls (EZ Pass)
Fuel, mileage, or other costs to operate a personal vehicle or taxi
Non-work related transportation or parking
Travel from office to business or client meetings
Transit or parking expenses of your spouse or dependents

chapters.title: Life Insurance
chapters.description: Basic Life and AD&D provided at no cost, plus voluntary supplemental coverage for you and your dependents through Mutual of Omaha.
contentParagraphs: RS&H provides Basic Life Insurance and Accidental Death & Dismemberment (AD&D) at no cost in the amount of your annual salary (up to $1,000,000) through Mutual of Omaha.
Although coverage is automatic, you must designate a beneficiary. Please keep your beneficiary designation up to date.
At age 65, your basic life insurance coverage begins to reduce: 35% reduction at 65, 45% at 70, 70% at 75, and 80% at 80.

rows.label: Family — Child Only
cells: 15% of your total AD&D coverage per child

chapters.title: Disability Insurance
chapters.description: Short-term and long-term disability coverage through Mutual of Omaha — protecting your income when you can't work.
contentParagraphs: Short-Term Disability (STD) Core Plan is provided by RS&H at no cost. Benefits begin after 7 calendar days and pay 60% of income up to $3,500/week for up to 180 days.
A STD Buy-Up Plan is available for purchase, providing an additional 30% of income (up to $2,000/week). Buy-Up benefits are tax-free income.
Long-Term Disability (LTD) is provided at no cost. Benefits begin after 180 calendar days and pay 60% of income up to $20,000/month until Social Security Normal Retirement Age.

chapters.title: Voluntary Benefits
chapters.description: Supplemental insurance options including Accident Insurance and Critical Illness/Cancer coverage through Mutual of Omaha.
contentParagraphs: Voluntary benefits are designed to protect your family's finances in case of an unforeseen injury or illness. These benefits are offered through Mutual of Omaha.
You may take these policies with you if you retire or leave RS&H.

sections.title: Accident Insurance Highlights
paragraphs: Family coverage available; no medical questions required
Coverage applies any time of day — on or off the job
Pays per covered accident with no limit on number of accidents
Portable: take the policy with you if you leave RS&H
$50 Health Screening Benefit per person per year

sections.title: Critical Illness & Cancer Covered Conditions
paragraphs: Cancer
Heart Attack
Stroke
Coronary Bypass Surgery
Major Organ Transplant
Advanced Parkinson's / Alzheimer's
End Stage Renal Failure
Childhood and Developmental Conditions
And MORE!

chapters.title: 401(k) Retirement Savings Plan
chapters.description: Save for retirement with pre-tax or Roth contributions and a 50% RS&H company match on up to 6% of pay.
contentParagraphs: The RS&H 401(k) plan lets you contribute part of your salary toward retirement while deferring taxes. A Roth 401(k) option is also available.

All new associates are automatically enrolled at 6% of pay (pre-tax) into T. Rowe Price Target Date Retirement Funds. You have ~45 days from hire to opt out or change elections via NetBenefits.com or 1-800-835-5095.
RS&H matches 50% of your contributions up to 6% of pay. Of the match, 2/3 is contributed in cash each pay period and 1/3 is contributed in company stock annually.
All associates over age 18 — including temporary and part-time — are eligible to participate.

chapters.title: Employee Assistance Program (EAP)
chapters.description: Free, confidential counseling and life navigation services available 24/7 to all associates and their families through Health Advocate.
contentParagraphs: RS&H offers all associates and their families access to a confidential Employee Assistance Program (EAP) through Health Advocate — available 24/7.
Six free visits (virtual or in person) per incident with licensed professionals. Phone, face-to-face, and web-based assistance available.
Contact: 1-866-799-2728 | www.healthadvocate.com/members | Plan: RS&H

sections.title: EAP Services Include
paragraphs: Stress, depression, and substance abuse counseling
Anxiety, grief, and eating disorder support
Child and elder care navigation
Debt, bankruptcy, and financial counseling
Divorce, child custody, and family law guidance
Wills, estate planning, and legal referrals
Adoption, pregnancy, and military life support
Cancer, heart health, and chronic condition coaching
And much more

chapters.title: Additional Voluntary Benefits
chapters.description: Identity theft protection, pet insurance, legal plan, and auto insurance available through payroll deduction.
contentParagraphs: RS&H offers a range of additional voluntary benefits to support your everyday life — all available through convenient payroll deduction.

sections.title: Identity Theft Protection — Allstate
paragraphs: Up to $1 million reimbursement per person / $2 million per family
Tri-bureau credit, dark web, and social media monitoring
24/7 fraud recovery alerts (email, mobile, app)
Military-grade VPN, Password Manager, and personal device protection
Monthly cost: Associate Only $9.50 | Associate + Family $17.50
Contact: 1-800-789-2720 | www.myaip.com/rsandh

sections.title: Pet Insurance — PetPartners
paragraphs: Comprehensive coverage for accidents, illnesses, injuries, and cancer for dogs and cats
Enroll anytime at www.petpartners.com/enroll?p=RSandH — use discount code RSH10
Contact: 1-866-774-1113 | help@petpartners.com

sections.title: Legal Plan — MetLife MetLaw
paragraphs: Covers associate, spouse, and dependents for a flat bi-weekly fee of $10.38
Unlimited telephone and office consultations on personal legal matters
Services: estate planning, immigration, document review, elder law, family law
Contact: 1-800-821-6400 | info.legalplans.com

sections.title: Auto Insurance — Farmers GroupSelect
paragraphs: Discounted group rates through Farmers GroupSelect
Paid via convenient payroll deductions; coverage stays with you if you change jobs

chapters.title: Paid Time Off & Other Benefits
chapters.description: Vacation, holidays, personal leave, paid parental leave, bereavement, and other time-off programs at RS&H.
contentParagraphs: RS&H observes 8 paid holidays annually plus one floating holiday per accrual year.
Vacation is credited based on tenure, with up to 20 days per year for associates with 20+ years of service.
Personal Leave (PL) is available for personal illness, family emergencies, medical appointments, and other situations. Unused PL transfers to a Leave Bank (max 240 hours) for qualified FMLA leaves.

sections.title: Observed Holidays
paragraphs: New Year's Day
Martin Luther King Jr. Day
Memorial Day
Independence Day
Labor Day
Thanksgiving
Day after Thanksgiving
Christmas Day
Plus one Floating Holiday per accrual year

sections.title: Paid Parental Leave
paragraphs: Up to 2 weeks of paid parental leave for the birth (including surrogacy) or adoption of a child age 17 and under
Associates with accrued Leave Bank hours may use up to 40 additional hours (1 week) for a total of 3 weeks
Available to all benefit-eligible associates

sections.title: Other Leave Programs
paragraphs: Bereavement Leave: up to 3 days for immediate family members
Jury Duty: paid leave for the duration of jury service
Flexible Work Arrangements: available where applicable

chapters.title: Benefit Resources & Contacts
chapters.description: Glossary of key benefit terms and complete contact directory for all RS&H benefit carriers.
contentParagraphs: The following glossary defines common benefit terms. Use the contact directory below to reach your benefit carriers directly.

sections.title: Glossary of Key Terms
paragraphs: Copayment (Copay): Fixed dollar amount you pay for certain in-network services on a PPO plan.
Coinsurance: Your percentage share of costs after meeting the deductible (e.g., you pay 20%, plan pays 80%).
Deductible: The amount you must pay before your plan begins paying coinsurance. Copay services typically don't apply to the deductible.
Out-of-Pocket Maximum: The most you will pay in a plan year; after this the plan pays 100% of covered benefits.
Formulary: The list of prescription drugs covered by the plan.
In-Network: Providers who have a negotiated rate agreement with your plan — lower cost to you.
Out-of-Network: Providers without a plan agreement — you pay more and may face balance billing.
Balance Billing: When an out-of-network provider bills you for the difference between their rate and what insurance pays.
